<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
char = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] == 40][0]
yellow_L = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] == 45][0]
dark_gray = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'c'][0]

print(f"Char: cols={char['boundary'][0][1]}-{char['boundary'][1][1]}, rows={char['boundary'][0][0]}-{char['boundary'][2][0]}")
print(f"Yellow L: cols={yellow_L['boundary'][0][1]}-{yellow_L['boundary'][1][1]}")
print(f"Dark gray: pixels={dark_gray['pixels']}")

# Check all yellow objects
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'Y':
        print(f"Yellow obj: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:3]}")

# Char is at cols ~39-47, yellow L at cols ~51-59
# Move RIGHT to overlap with yellow L
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
# Find the character by looking for black objects with white children
for n in seg:
    if n['children']:
        children_colors = [seg[c]['color'] for c in n['children']]
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, children={children_colors}")

# The character should be the one with white dot children
# Let me check id=3
n3 = seg[3]
print(f"\nid=3: color={n3['color']}, pixels={n3['pixels']}, children={n3['children']}")
print(f"boundary={n3['boundary']}")

# Check if there's a separate character object
# Maybe the character is id=3 with the white dots as children
# Let me see if I can move it

# Try moving LEFT to get back from the edge
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
